title: JavaScript Page Redirects Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of window.location, assign(), and replace() in JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct syntax for simple URL redirection?
options: window.location.href = "url"
window.location.assign("url")
Both A and B are valid.
Neither A nor B.

questions.question: What is the key difference between location.assign() and location.replace()?
options: location.replace() clears the current page from the browser's history.
location.assign() clears the current page from the browser's history.
There is no difference.
Only location.replace() works in all browsers.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the location.reload() method?
options: To redirect the user to a new URL.
To reload the current page.
To clear the browser's history.
To check the user's connection status.

questions.question: Which property can you use to get the full URL of the current page?
options: window.location.href
window.location.origin
window.location.pathname
window.location.host

questions.question: In an HTML meta redirect, what does the content="5;url=..." attribute do?
options: It redirects the user after 5 seconds.
It redirects the user only if the page takes 5 seconds to load.
It sets a timer to refresh the page every 5 seconds.
It sets the width of the redirect page to 5 pixels.
